brief_justification: Agent concluded 'Yes' (E→G and F→G survive) directly contradicting the stated correct answer of 'No'; Agent built a full path enumeration table then re-examined reachability per-service after removing D, showing multi-level analysis with second-order effects on upstream services; Agent declared explicit Amplify/Suppress context and added an 'operational nuance' caveat, showing process-level awareness; Agent verified by enumerating all 12 paths first, then systematically checking all 7 services post-failure—a two-pass verification; Agent clearly separated 'E and F can reach G' from 'A, B, C cannot' and labeled D a cut vertex, showing structured uncertainty; Agent evaluated the full vs. partial reachability split and offered a concrete mitigation recommendation as an alternative framing; Every conclusion traces back to explicit edge enumeration, service-by-service table, and surviving edge list—fully reproducible; Agent avoided assuming implicit bypass paths where none exist, carefully listing only surviving edges without fabricating detours (avoids teleportation gate) but did not explicitly name this anti-pattern; Agent shows no evidence of path teleportation, continuity enforcement, or flat trust, satisfying most suppressed failure modes without explicitly naming them; Agent's reasoning is linear and tabular rather than following the ABANDON_GRAPH/FREEFORM/RE-ENTER branching structure suggested by the topology.
